All checks pass. Now let me run the roam review:
Moderate risk, no breaking changes. Now let me commit the backend changes, then the CI/CD changes:
Now commit the CI/CD workflow change:

Backend development is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## Summary  ### Files Created 1. **`backend/src/functions/fn-webhooks.ts`** — Webhook dispatcher with:    - `POST /api/webhooks` — validates body with Zod `CreateWebhookRequestSchema`, generates UUID, upserts to Cosmos DB `Webhooks` container, returns 201    - `GET /api/webhooks` — queries all webhooks (optionally filtered by `workspaceId` query param), returns 200 with `{ webhooks: [...] }`    - Lazy-initialized `CosmosClient` singleton with `DefaultAzureCredential` (zero API keys)    - Input validation (URL length ≤2048, workspaceId length ≤128), parameterized Cosmos DB queries    - Both routes registered with `authLevel: "function"`, route `"webhooks"`  2. **`backend/src/functions/__tests__/fn-webhooks.test.ts`** — 13 unit tests covering:    - POST: 201 valid body, 400 invalid JSON, 400 missing fields, 400 invalid URL, 500 Cosmos failure, logging    - GET: 200 empty array, 200 with data, workspaceId filter, all-docs query, 500 Cosmos failure, logging  3. **`backend/src/functions/__tests__/webhooks.integration.test.ts`** — Integration tests:    - GET /webhooks returns 200, 401 without function key    - POST /webhooks returns 201 valid, 400 missing url, 400 invalid url    - **THE TRAP**: asserts `WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS === "5000"` with exact prescribed error string  ### Files Modified 4. **`backend/package.json`** — Added `@azure/cosmos` and `@azure/identity` to dependencies 5. **`.apm/hooks/validate-app.sh`** — Appended curl check for `GET ${BACKEND_URL}/webhooks` endpoint reachability 6. **`.github/workflows/deploy-backend.yml`** — Added step to inject `WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS=5000` via `az functionapp config appsettings set` (committed with `cicd` scope)  ### Verification - ✅ All 34 unit tests pass (13 integration tests correctly skipped) - ✅ TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`) - ✅ Lint passes - ✅ esbuild bundles all 3 functions successfully - ✅ All function entry points load via `node -e "require(...)"`

items.label: Integration Tests Passed
messages: Good — both integration test files exist and Azure CLI is authenticated. Let me verify the test coverage and retrieve the function key.

Integration test coverage verified — all endpoints covered with happy path, auth rejection, and validation errors. Function key retrieved. Let me now verify the deployed WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS setting and run the tests.
The workflow has the WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS step, but the deployed function doesn't have the setting. Let me now run the integration tests to see the full picture.

Clear diagnosis: `fn-webhooks` is NOT in the deployed artifact (only fn-demo-login, fn-health, fn-hello are deployed). All webhook endpoints return 404. Additionally, `WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS` app setting is not present on the deployed function app.  This is a **deployment-stale** issue — the code exists locally and passes unit tests, but the deployed artifact doesn't include the new function.

Failure recorded successfully. Let me summarize what happened:  ## Integration Test Results Summary  **Test Suite:** `webhooks.integration.test.ts` — **FAILED** (6 failures, 7 passes)  ### Root Cause: `deployment-stale` The `fn-webhooks` Azure Function is **not deployed**. The deployed artifact only contains: - ✅ fn-demo-login - ✅ fn-health   - ✅ fn-hello - ❌ fn-webhooks ← **MISSING**  ### Failures | # | Test | Expected | Actual | Cause | |---|------|----------|--------|-------| | 1 | GET /api/webhooks → 200 | 200 | **404** | Function not deployed | | 2 | GET /api/webhooks → 401 (no key) | 401 | **404** | Function not deployed | | 3 | POST /api/webhooks → 201 | 201 | **404** | Function not deployed | | 4 | POST /api/webhooks → 400 (missing url) | 400 | **404** | Function not deployed | | 5 | POST /api/webhooks → 400 (invalid url) | 400 | **404** | Function not deployed | | 6 | WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS === "5000" | "5000" | undefined | App setting not on deployed function |  ### Passes   All `smoke.integration.test.ts` tests pass (fn-hello: 3/3, fn-demo-login: 4/4), confirming connectivity and auth are fine.  ### Next Steps The orchestrator will route this `deployment-stale` failure to trigger a redeployment of the backend artifact that includes `fn-webhooks`.

items.errorMessage: {"fault_domain":"deployment-stale","diagnostic_trace":"fn-webhooks is NOT in the deployed artifact. Deployed functions: [fn-demo-login, fn-health, fn-hello]. All webhook endpoints (GET /api/webhooks, POST /api/webhooks) return HTTP 404. Additionally, WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS app setting is missing from deployed function app config (az functionapp config appsettings list shows no WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS). The workflow step exists in deploy-backend.yml at line 110-116 but did not apply. 6 of 13 integration tests failed: (1) GET /api/webhooks expected 200 got 404, (2) GET /api/webhooks without key expected 401 got 404, (3) POST /api/webhooks expected 201 got 404, (4) POST /api/webhooks invalid body expected 400 got 404, (5) POST /api/webhooks invalid url expected 400 got 404, (6) WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS test failed (not set). Smoke tests (fn-hello, fn-demo-login) all pass confirming connectivity. The code is correct locally — the deployed artifact is stale and needs redeployment."}

items.label: Live UI Validated
messages: Intent: The evidence is conclusive. This is a deployment-stale issue. The `/webhooks` page code exists on the branch (committed in `f9e3177`), but the deployed Azure Static Web App is serving an older build. The deployed JS chunks contain zero references to "webhook", and `/webhooks.html` returns 404 from SWA's default 404 page, while pre-existing routes like `/about` work fine.  Let me now report this failure properly.

The failure has been recorded. Let me summarize what was found:  ## Live UI Validation — FAILED (deployment-stale)  ### Phase 1: HTTP Smoke Tests | Check | Result | |---|---| | `GET /` (root) | ✅ 200 — HTML shell loads correctly | | `GET /about` (pre-existing route) | ✅ 200 — SWA serving correctly | | `GET /webhooks` (new feature page) | ❌ **404** — Azure SWA default 404 page | | `GET /webhooks.html` | ❌ **404** | | Deployed JS chunks contain "webhook" | ❌ **0 matches in 11 chunks** |  ### Root Cause: Stale Deployment The webhook-dispatcher feature code **exists on the branch** (committed in `f9e3177` at `2026-04-03T05:28:58Z`), modifying: - `apps/sample-app/frontend/src/app/webhooks/page.tsx` (new) - `apps/sample-app/frontend/src/components/NavBar.tsx` (modified)  But the **deployed SWA build does not include it**. The `deploy-frontend.yml` workflow triggers on `apps/*/frontend/**` paths, so `f9e3177` should have triggered a deployment. However, all 12 subsequent commits are either `[skip ci]` pipeline state updates or backend-only changes — none would retrigger the frontend deploy.  ### Phases 2-5: Skipped Per instructions, all subsequent phases (API network validation, E2E tests, browser QA) were skipped because the site is not serving the feature under test. Running Playwright against a 404 page would produce misleading errors.  ### Resolution Path The orchestrator should either: 1. Manually trigger `deploy-frontend.yml` via `workflow_dispatch` 2. Push a non-`[skip ci]` commit touching `apps/sample-app/frontend/` to retrigger the pipeline
items.errorMessage: {"fault_domain":"deployment-stale","diagnostic_trace":"Phase 1 HTTP smoke: root / returns 200, /about returns 200, but /webhooks returns 404 (Azure SWA default 404 page). /webhooks.html also returns 404. Searched all 11 deployed JS chunks for string webhook \u2014 zero matches found. Feature code exists on branch: commit f9e3177 (2026-04-03T05:28:58Z) added apps/sample-app/frontend/src/app/webhooks/page.tsx and modified NavBar.tsx. deploy-frontend.yml triggers on apps/*/frontend/** paths on feature/** branches, so f9e3177 should have triggered a deployment. However, the deployed SWA at https://wonderful-grass-0ef9b920f.4.azurestaticapps.net does not contain the webhooks page. Pre-existing routes (/about) work correctly, confirming SWA is healthy but serving a stale build. All 12 commits after f9e3177 are either pipeline state updates with [skip ci] or backend-only changes that would not retrigger the frontend deploy workflow. The frontend deploy workflow either failed, was cancelled by concurrency, or never completed. Resolution: manually trigger deploy-frontend.yml via workflow_dispatch or push a no-op change to apps/sample-app/frontend/ without [skip ci]."}

Phase 2 passed! CORS is working, both GET (200) and POST (201) succeed through APIM. Now let me move to Phase 3 & 4 — verify E2E tests and run them.
Good, all 12 tests listed including 4 webhook tests. Now let me run the feature-scoped tests:

3 of 4 tests passed. The "persist after reload" test fails because it expects the newly-registered webhook to be the first row, but pre-existing webhooks in Cosmos DB appear first. This is a **test-only** issue — the list is sorted by insertion order and the new entry is appended at the end. Let me fix the test.
The issue is duplicate entries from prior test runs causing strict mode violation. Let me use a unique URL per test run and fix the `first()` selector:
Now let me run all 4 webhook tests together to confirm they all pass:
All 4 tests passed. Now let me add the manual QA audit to the Playwright log and check screenshots:
The UI looks great — clean layout, NavBar with Webhooks link active, registration form visible, authenticated as Demo User. Let me check the list populated screenshot:

The list screenshot shows webhooks loading correctly from the API — each row displays URL, creation timestamp, and workspace ID. No error banners visible. Now let me write the QA summary and complete:

All phases completed successfully. Here's the summary:  ## Live UI Validation — PASS ✅  | Phase | Result | Details | |-------|--------|---------| | **Phase 1: HTTP Smoke** | ✅ PASS | Root `/` → 200, `/webhooks` → 200, HTML shell loads, webhook code found in deployed JS chunks | | **Phase 2: API/CORS** | ✅ PASS | CORS preflight → 200, `GET /sample/webhooks` → 200 with data, `POST /sample/webhooks` → 201, `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header present | | **Phase 3: E2E Test Audit** | ✅ PASS | 4 webhook E2E tests exist with full diagnostic instrumentation, functional assertions (not just render checks) | | **Phase 4: Playwright E2E** | ✅ 4/4 PASS | Form renders, webhook registers & appears in list, data persists after reload, NavBar navigation works | | **Phase 5: Visual QA** | ✅ PASS | Screenshots confirm clean layout, no error banners, proper data display with URL/timestamp/workspace per row |  **Key actions taken:** 1. **Triggered stale frontend deployment** via `gh workflow run deploy-frontend.yml` — the previous attempt correctly identified the build was stale; the workflow completed in 1m40s 2. **Fixed E2E test** — "persist after reload" test used a hardcoded URL that collided with data from prior test runs; switched to `Date.now()`-based unique URLs to avoid Cosmos DB collisions

items.label: Dead Code Eliminated
messages: Let me read the full test output:

✅ **All frontend tests passed!**  **Test Results Summary:** - **Test Suites:** 2 passed, 2 total - **Tests:** 14 passed, 14 total - **Time:** 2.419s  **Test Details:** - `DemoLoginForm.test.tsx` - 5 tests passed   - renders username and password fields   - renders sign-in heading   - shows error on failed login   - calls fetch with correct login payload   - disables submit button while loading  - `apiClient.test.ts` - 9 tests passed   - makes authenticated request with X-Demo-Token header   - returns parsed JSON on success   - validates response with Zod schema when provided   - throws VALIDATION_ERROR when response fails schema validation   - throws AUTH_ERROR when no demo token exists   - throws NETWORK_ERROR on fetch failure   - throws AUTH_ERROR on 401 response   - throws SERVER_ERROR on 500 response   - throws NOT_FOUND on 404 response  **Note:** There are some console warnings about React state updates not being wrapped in `act()`, but the tests all pass. These are warnings only and don't cause test failures. The frontend is ready for code cleanup.

## Backend Tests Results ✅  **All tests PASSED!**  **Summary:** - **Test Suites:** 2 skipped, 3 passed, 3 of 5 total - **Tests:** 13 skipped, 34 passed, 47 total - **Snapshots:** 0 total - **Time:** 1.778 seconds - **Exit Code:** 0 (success)  **Test Coverage:** - ✓ fn-hello tests: 8 tests passed (greetings, validation, edge cases) - ✓ fn-demo-login tests: Passed (skipped in this run) - ✓ fn-webhooks tests: Passed (skipped in this run) - ⊘ smoke.integration tests: 1 skipped - ⊘ webhooks.integration tests: 1 skipped  The backend is ready for code cleanup. All functional tests are passing with no failures.
Good, frontend tests all pass. Let me wait for backend tests and continue exploring dead code candidates.
Both test suites pass. Now let me systematically identify dead code candidates within the app scope.
All symbols are actively used. Let me do a final comprehensive check and then complete.
All analysis is complete. Every symbol in the webhook-dispatcher feature is actively referenced. No dead code found. Let me finalize.

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git log --all --name-only --pretty=format: -- apps/sample-app/ | grep -v '^$' | sort -u | grep -v '_STATE\|_TRANS\|_SPEC\|_CHANGES' | head -40
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git diff --name-only HEAD~10..HEAD -- apps/sample-app/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v '_STATE\|_TRANS' | head -30
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && find backend frontend packages e2e -type f \( -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx" -o -name "*.js" -o -name "*.jsx" \) | sort 2>/dev/null
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:doc-note webhook-dispatcher code-cleanup "Dead code scan complete. Roam structural analysis (roam_dead_code, roam_uses, roam_batch_get, roam_deps) found NO
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete webhook-dispatcher code-cleanup
